title: Nycteranthus commutatus
taxonomy: Nycteranthus commutatus is classified within the family Portulacaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the purslane or moss-rose family. This plant is also placed in the order Caryophyllales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The family Portulacaceae is native to the Americas, Africa, and Asia, and is characterized by its succulent leaves and stems. Nycteranthus commutatus is a species of flowering plant endemic to South Africa. The classification of Nycteranthus commutatus is further refined at the genus and species levels, where it is a member of the genus Nycteranthus.
